diagnosis: e)  Three or more consecutive ectopic (non-sinus) P waves are present at a rapid rate.  The patient had an exacerbation of severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), a common substrate in patients with multifocal atrial tachycardia (MAT).  In contrast, wandering atrial pacemaker (WAP) is a slower rhythm, usually with more gradual change in P waves.  (Note that some of the ectopic P waves are non-conducted.)
